name: Rive (Interactive Animation Runtime)
pricing_notes: Rive editor: free/paid tiers; Runtime SDK: open source (MIT); .riv file format

auth.notes: Local runtime; Rive editor account for cloud collaboration

performance.notes: WebAssembly + WebGL2; GPU-accelerated; extremely small runtime (~400KB); 60fps

agent_readiness.notes: Next-generation interactive animation platform. Rive editor (browser-based) creates .riv files. State machine for interactive animations responding to user input. Data binding for dynamic content. React, iOS, Android, Flutter, C++ runtimes. WebGL2 renderer via WASM. Live synchronization of text/images in animations. Game-quality animations for UI. More capable than Lottie: interactive state machines, not just playback. Used by Google Classroom, Duolingo.

gotchas: Proprietary .riv format — tied to Rive editor
Learning curve for Rive editor state machine paradigm
WASM runtime is heavier than Lottie's pure JS
Editor collaboration features require paid plan
best_when: Interactive animations responding to user input (buttons, sliders, toggles)
avoid_when: Applications where JS-only runtime is required
summary: Rive is a next-generation interactive animation platform. .riv files created in Rive editor (browser-based) include state machines for interactive animations responding to user input. WebGL2 + WASM runtime. Cross-platform: web, iOS, Android, Flutter. More capable than Lottie (interactive state machines vs simple playback). Used by Google Classroom and Duolingo. Best for interactive, responsive UI animations.
